body:
## Summary
- Collapse older same-agent inline PR review comments when posting a new review synthesis.
- Record synthesis start time so inline comments posted by the current run remain visible.
- Update docs and focused regression coverage.

## Verification
- `npm run build`
- `node --test dist/__tests__/review-summary-minimize.test.js dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js dist/__tests__/post-comment-cli.test.js`

Closes #67

comments.body:
Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| implement | review | PR #163 | 3 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced open PR #163 for issue #67; an automated review is the useful next step before human merge.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1778146614962 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjU0ODc2ODEzOTY6aW1wbGVtZW50OjY3OnJldmlldzoxNjM6Mw -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 96 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The change extends the existing typed minimization helper in [`review-summary-minimize.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.agent/src/review-summary-minimize.ts) and wires it through the existing [`post-comment.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.agent/src/cli/post-comment.ts) path, rather than adding workflow-only GitHub API logic. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | The PR updates both [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md) and [`configuration-list.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md) to mention same-agent inline review comment cleanup. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | partial | 5/7 | The workflow adds a separate `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` value and passes it explicitly to the CLI. However, [`post-comment.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.agent/src/cli/post-comment.ts) parses missing or malformed values as `0`, which silently disables inline cleanup rather than surfacing a clear route-specific warning or validation failure. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR #163 closes #67, which explicitly links related umbrella issue #65. The implementation matches #67’s behavior request while keeping #65’s broader refactor out of scope. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to the review cleanup helper, the post-comment CLI wiring, the review workflow timestamp, focused tests, and matching docs. No broad refactor or unrelated cleanup is included. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | The change keeps the latest review artifacts visible and minimizes older same-agent generated inline comments as outdated, preserving expandable history instead of creating unmanaged duplicate visible output. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation adds the minimum new GraphQL queries and pagination needed for inline review comments, reusing the existing actor matching and minimization mutation. |

## Notes

- Main score drivers are positive: the PR is focused, uses the existing typed minimization path, includes regression coverage, and updates the relevant docs.
- The only rubric-specific follow-up is to make `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` validation more explicit for review cleanup, so a broken timestamp does not quietly skip the newly added inline-comment cleanup behavior.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` is treated as optional everywhere, so if the workflow output is missing or malformed, inline review comment cleanup silently becomes a no-op while the review post still succeeds. This slightly weakens the “Prefer explicit structured inputs” rubric. Evidence: the PR adds the env value in `.github/workflows/agent-review.yml`, but `post-comment.ts` uses `Number(process.env.CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS || "0")`, and `review-summary-minimize.ts` only fetches inline review comments when the parsed value is finite and greater than zero.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #163 addresses issue #67 by collapsing older same-agent inline PR review comments when a new review synthesis runs.
- It extends the existing generated-comment minimization path instead of adding separate workflow-only GitHub API logic.
- The workflow now records a review-synthesis cutoff timestamp and passes it into `.agent/src/cli/post-comment.ts`.
- The broader helper/API refactor remains tracked by #65, and this PR intentionally keeps to the concrete #67 behavior.

## Review
The reviewers found no blocking defects, and the PR is directionally sound. The remaining issues are concrete, branch-scoped polish around timestamp correctness, validation, and focused coverage.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Runner-clock cutoff can misclassify inline comments | WARNING | `.github/workflows/agent-review.yml` records the cutoff with runner-local `Date.now()` but compares it to GitHub `createdAt` timestamps. Clock skew can hide current-run inline comments or leave stale comments visible. |
| Inline review thread pagination lacks regression coverage | WARNING | The new fetch path paginates both review threads and per-thread comments, but the added test only covers a one-page response. Cursor regressions could silently leave old inline comments uncollapsed. |
| Malformed cutoff env silently disables inline cleanup | WARNING | `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` is parsed as optional and invalid values become a quiet no-op for inline cleanup. The review post still succeeds without surfacing that the new behavior was skipped. |

## Progress
- Docs are updated for `AGENT_COLLAPSE_OLD_REVIEWS` and same-agent inline review cleanup.
- Reviewers reported `npm run build` and focused node tests passing.
- The rubrics review already acknowledged the malformed timestamp handling concern in the PR conversation.
- Inline comments were posted for the runner timestamp source and pagination coverage gaps.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Runner-clock cutoff can misclassify inline comments</summary>

**Cause:** [`agent-review.yml#L276`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.github/workflows/agent-review.yml#L276) uses runner-local `Date.now()`, while inline review comment timestamps come from GitHub.

**Candidate solutions:** Derive the cutoff from GitHub server time, such as workflow-run metadata or an API response timestamp, before comparing against GraphQL `createdAt`.

**Comments:** Codex rated this WARNING; Claude noted it as lower practical risk. Synthesis keeps it as WARNING because self-hosted runner skew can violate the “current review stays visible” guarantee. Inline: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163#discussion_r3200455526

</details>

<details>
<summary>Inline review thread pagination lacks regression coverage</summary>

**Cause:** [`review-summary-minimize.test.ts#L159`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.agent/src/__tests__/review-summary-minimize.test.ts#L159) tests filtering on one page, while [`review-summary-minimize.ts#L383`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.agent/src/review-summary-minimize.ts#L383) has outer and inner pagination loops.

**Candidate solutions:** Add focused queued-client tests for `reviewThreads.pageInfo.hasNextPage` and per-thread `comments.pageInfo.hasNextPage`, asserting cursor variables and minimized IDs.

**Comments:** Inline: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163#discussion_r3200456918

</details>

<details>
<summary>Malformed cutoff env silently disables inline cleanup</summary>

**Cause:** [`post-comment.ts#L34`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.agent/src/cli/post-comment.ts#L34) parses missing values as `0`, and [`review-summary-minimize.ts#L454`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/4b6f08953b278b82a38dfb4d96ac1647b1e42618/.agent/src/review-summary-minimize.ts#L454) skips inline cleanup unless the cutoff is finite and positive.

**Candidate solutions:** Validate the raw env value for review cleanup and either fail clearly or log a route-specific warning when it is missing or malformed. Add focused CLI coverage for the failure mode.

**Comments:** Already covered by the posted rubrics review, so no duplicate inline comment was added.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The unresolved findings are concrete workflow/test/validation changes and are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Replace the runner-local `Date.now()` cutoff with a GitHub-server-derived timestamp.
- [ ] Validate or warn on missing/malformed `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` instead of silently skipping inline cleanup.
- [ ] Add regression tests for outer `reviewThreads` pagination and inner per-thread comment pagination.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| review | fix-pr | PR #163 | 4 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis for PR #163 recommends FIX_PR with MINOR_ISSUES; the remaining items are concrete branch changes within the current PR and round budget remains available.

Task for fix-pr:
Address only the latest review synthesis action items on PR #163:
- Replace the runner-local `Date.now()` cutoff with a GitHub-server-derived timestamp.
- Validate or warn on missing/malformed `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` instead of silently skipping inline cleanup.
- Add regression tests for outer `reviewThreads` pagination and inner per-thread comment pagination.

Constraints: Ignore optional INFO notes, metadata-only polish, already-fixed findings, and human-judgment nits unless required by those action items. Keep the scope aligned with issue #67 as a concrete behavior case related to umbrella #65, without doing the broader #65 refactor.

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-67/codex-25487681396`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Replaced the review cleanup cutoff’s runner-local `Date.now()` with a GitHub Actions run `run_started_at` timestamp from the GitHub API.
- Added review CLI warnings for missing or malformed `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` so inline cleanup is not skipped silently.
- Added regression coverage for outer `reviewThreads` pagination and inner per-thread comment pagination.
- Verification passed: `npm run build`; `node --test dist/__tests__/review-summary-minimize.test.js dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js dist/__tests__/post-comment-cli.test.js`.

| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md` and `.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md` for same-agent inline review comment cleanup. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds a distinct `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` value, wires it explicitly from `agent-review.yml`, and validates missing/malformed values with focused CLI warnings and tests. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR closes #67, which references #65. The diff implements the bounded #67 behavior without taking on the broader #65 refactor. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Scope is limited to review cleanup logic, workflow timestamp plumbing, focused tests, and matching docs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | New cleanup minimizes older same-agent inline review comments while preserving the newest run’s comments and expandable history. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Uses straightforward GraphQL pagination and the existing minimization mutation, with targeted regression coverage for thread and comment pagination. |

## Notes

- The current PR head includes the follow-up fixes requested by the prior review: GitHub run metadata is used for the cutoff timestamp, malformed cutoff input now emits warnings, and pagination coverage was added.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #163 closes #67 by collapsing older same-agent inline PR review comments when a new review synthesis is posted.
- The work is a bounded behavior case related to umbrella issue #65, which remains open for the broader generated-comment minimization refactor.
- The PR extends the existing typed minimization helper, wires a synthesis cutoff through the review workflow, updates docs, and adds regression coverage.
- A prior review cycle found timestamp and pagination issues; the current branch has fixed those items.

## Review
The current implementation is mostly sound, and Codex plus the rubrics review found no remaining issues. Claude identified one concrete workflow reliability regression that should be fixed before merge.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `synthesis_start` failure can block review posting | WARNING | The new timestamp step runs on the critical path without `continue-on-error: true`; a transient `gh api` or parse failure stops the review job before synthesis is posted. |

## Progress
- Previous runner-clock cutoff, malformed timestamp handling, and pagination coverage findings are addressed on head commit `5600175d5f5aba7e5b5eb52da1e659b4b2046077`.
- Docs are updated for same-agent inline review comment cleanup.
- Reviewers reported `npm run build`, focused node tests, and `npm test` passing.
- A new inline comment was posted for the remaining workflow reliability issue: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163#discussion_r3200576042

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>`synthesis_start` failure can block review posting</summary>

**Cause:** [`agent-review.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/5600175d5f5aba7e5b5eb52da1e659b4b2046077/.github/workflows/agent-review.yml#L274-L282) fetches `run_started_at` with `set -euo pipefail`, but the step is not best-effort. If the API call or timestamp parse fails, the job exits before the synthesis and post-comment steps run.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `continue-on-error: true` to the `Record synthesis start time` step. The existing post-comment path already warns and skips inline cleanup when `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` is missing, so this gives the intended fallback.

**Comments:** Codex and the rubrics review found no current issues; Claude raised this as the only actionable concern. Synthesis keeps it as WARNING because it can turn an optional cleanup timestamp into a full review-route failure.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The remaining issue is a small, concrete workflow change that is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add `continue-on-error: true` to the `Record synthesis start time` workflow step.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #163 closes #67 by collapsing older same-agent inline PR review comments when a new review synthesis is posted.
- This is a bounded behavior case related to umbrella issue #65, which remains open for the broader generated-comment minimization refactor.
- The PR extends the existing typed minimization helper, wires a GitHub-server-derived synthesis cutoff through the review workflow, updates docs, and adds focused regression coverage.
- Prior review findings around runner-local time, malformed cutoff handling, pagination coverage, and best-effort timestamp lookup have been fixed on the current head.

## Review
The implementation is close, but one workflow contract issue remains. Codex flagged that the existing inline-comment duplicate check can conflict with the new post-synthesis cleanup; Claude and the rubrics review otherwise judged the current branch as sound.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Cleanup can hide repeated inline findings | WARNING | The synthesis prompt skips posting a new inline comment when an older one already covers the same file/line, but the post step can then collapse that older same-agent comment. A repeated current finding can end up with no visible inline comment. |
| Missing cutoff warning wording is imprecise | INFO | Claude noted that the missing `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` warning may also mean the best-effort timestamp step failed. This is operator clarity only and not required branch-change work. |

## Progress
- The earlier `Date.now()` cutoff issue is fixed by using GitHub Actions `run_started_at`.
- Missing or malformed `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS` now warns and skips inline cleanup instead of silently no-oping.
- Outer `reviewThreads` pagination and inner per-thread comment pagination now have regression coverage.
- The `Record synthesis start time` step now has `continue-on-error: true`.
- A new inline comment was posted for the remaining cleanup/dedupe contract issue: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163#discussion_r3200659337

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Cleanup can hide repeated inline findings</summary>

**Cause:** [`review-synthesize.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/65bfdaa9378b85b1d629a425d67bd964dbdcb34a/.github/prompts/review-synthesize.md#L16-L30) tells synthesis to skip inline comments already covered by existing inline feedback, while [`agent-review.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/65bfdaa9378b85b1d629a425d67bd964dbdcb34a/.github/workflows/agent-review.yml#L318-L325) runs cleanup after synthesis. Older same-agent inline comments can therefore suppress a fresh comment during synthesis and then be minimized during cleanup.

**Candidate solutions:** Update the synthesis/cleanup contract so duplicate checks ignore same-agent inline comments that are eligible for collapse, or move dedupe plus cleanup into a deterministic path that guarantees a current-run visible inline comment remains for repeated findings. Add focused coverage or prompt-level guardrails for the repeated-finding case.

**Comments:** Codex rated this WARNING; Claude did not flag it and otherwise considered the branch shippable. Synthesis keeps it as WARNING because it can break the user-facing purpose of inline review comments across repeated review rounds.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The remaining issue is a concrete workflow/prompt contract fix and is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Ensure repeated findings still get a visible current-run inline comment when older same-agent inline comments are eligible for cleanup.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

**Candidate solutions:** Pass a cleanup-enabled/cutoff signal into synthesis, or scope the prompt override so it only reposts repeated findings when cleanup will actually run. Apply the same contract to the finalize prompt and add prompt/envelope coverage.

**Comments:** Claude noted a softer prompt tension and still rated the PR shippable; Codex identified the cleanup-disabled and missing-cutoff cases as an unresolved WARNING. Synthesis keeps it as WARNING because it can leave duplicate visible inline feedback.

@sepo-agent /answer can you summarize main code changes and function here? 

For the comment collapse is it simply time based or is there agent support for closing them?

Main change: PR #163 extends generated-comment cleanup so a new review synthesis also collapses older same-agent **inline PR review comments**, not just older top-level review summaries.

Key files:
- [`.agent/src/review-summary-minimize.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163/files): adds GraphQL fetching for PR review threads and paginated thread comments, filters same-agent inline comments, then minimizes matching comments with GitHub’s `minimizeComment(... OUTDATED)`.
- [`.agent/src/cli/post-comment.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163/files): passes the review cutoff into cleanup and warns if the cutoff is missing/malformed.
- [`.github/workflows/agent-review.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163/files): records a GitHub-server-derived synthesis start timestamp and forwards it as `CURRENT_REVIEW_STARTED_AT_MS`.
- [review synthesis prompts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163/files): tell review synthesis when it may repost an inline finding despite duplicate detection.
- Tests/docs were updated for pagination, invalid cutoff handling, workflow wiring, and `AGENT_COLLAPSE_OLD_REVIEWS`.

For inline comment collapse, it is **both agent-supported and time-gated**:

- Agent support: Sepo actively calls GitHub GraphQL `minimizeComment` with classifier `OUTDATED`; it is not relying on GitHub automatically aging comments out.
- Time gate: inline comments are only cleanup-eligible if they were created before the current review synthesis started.
- Actor gate: only comments from the same authenticated agent account are collapsed.
- Safety gate: already-minimized comments and human comments are skipped.
- Config gate: `AGENT_COLLAPSE_OLD_REVIEWS=false` disables this cleanup.

So the timestamp is not “collapse anything older than N minutes.” It is a run boundary: “older than this review synthesis run.” The agent uses that boundary so current-run inline comments remain visible while stale same-agent inline comments from prior review rounds get collapsed.

title: Collapse older generated inline review comments
